summary: openmct-couch-plugin exfiltrates host identifiers to oastify.com on install
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle hook that shell-execs `curl` to POST the output of `whoami` and `hostname` to `http://8irluql1d21jmq8tr5n5fyoxjopfdd12.oastify.com` — a Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) out-of-band interaction host commonly used as attacker-controlled C2 / reconnaissance infrastructure. index.js additionally reads `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `process.cwd()`, and process version metadata, consistent with a host-fingerprinting payload. Installing this package on any machine causes unauthenticated outbound HTTP carrying installer identity to attacker infrastructure on `npm install`, before any user code runs and without consent. The endpoint and lifecycle-hook shape match the canonical preinstall-beacon supply-chain attack pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
